title: Ditassa colellae
taxonomy: Ditassa colellae is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It belongs to the tribe Amsonieae and the genus Ditassa. The genus Ditassa is part of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. The classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the plant family.

intro: Ditassa colellae is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ditassa colellae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ditassa colellae is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, rheumatism, and digestive problems.
